bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no opponent history yet and no time pressure to concede. I therefore choose the top value on each issue, especially holding firm on issueC and issueD because they carry the highest importance in my preferences.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02), and the opponent’s first offer gives me low utility while showing no concession pattern yet. Their cooperative message is noted, but I trust the numbers more than the appeal; I make only a tiny concession from my opening bid on low-weight issueA while holding my most important issueC and issueD at preferred values.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early (t=0.04) and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer rather than showing real concession. Their messages are cooperative/emotional, but the numbers show they are holding firm, so I will make only a small concession on a lower-weight issue while protecting my most important issueC and strong issueD preference.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only modest concessions on low-weight issues. The opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer for me and their messages are emotional appeals rather than concrete concessions, so I should not give up my high-weight priorities issueC and issueD. I will avoid repeating my exact previous bid and adjust a lower-weight issue instead.

I am still targeting a very high-utility bid because it is early (t=0.08) and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer to me without conceding. Their messages are cooperative/emotional, but the bid history shows no movement, so I will not make major concessions on my most important issues issueC and issueD. I am varying my offer slightly from my previous bid while preserving strong value on the issues that matter most to me.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making meaningful concessions on my lower-weight issues. The opponent has held firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC, but did concede issueD to my preferred valueC, so I will preserve issueD and mostly hold on high-importance issueC while offering their apparent preferences on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid (about 0.725 on my weighted scale) because it is early at t=0.12, so I should not give up my most important issue too much yet. The opponent’s actual offers show firmness on issueA/issueB/issueC and only a concession on issueD, so I am keeping issueD at the mutually signaled valueC while making only a small movement on high-weight issueC from valueC to valueD rather than accepting their much worse valueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74 by keeping my highest-weight issueC at valueC and preserving issueD at valueC, while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB to the values the opponent has consistently requested. Time is still early at t=0.14, and the opponent has only made a real concession on issueD while holding firm elsewhere, so I should not concede further on issueC yet despite their cooperative messages.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.725 by preserving my most important issue, issueC, near my preferred outcome while conceding fully on the lower-weight issues issueA and issueB. Since time is still early at t=0.16, I should not yet drop issueC to valueB, especially because the opponent has only meaningfully conceded on issueD and has otherwise held firm. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional appeals, so I am relying on the offer history rather than treating those claims as binding.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while preserving my most important issue, issueC, at valueC. The opponent has repeatedly held issueA and issueB at valueA and has conceded issueD to valueC, so I will match them on A/B and keep D at the mutually signaled valueC, while not yet conceding issueC down to valueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.725 by holding firm on the high-weight issueC while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB to the opponent’s repeated position. At t=0.20 there is still time, so I should not collapse to their latest low-utility bid, especially since their offers mostly hold firm and even worsened on issueC after previously conceding issueD. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the actual bid pattern shows they value issueA/issueB at valueA and have been willing to give issueD: valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.74 by holding firm on issueC and issueD, which are my most important issues, while conceding issueA and issueB to the opponent’s repeated preferred values. At t=0.22 there is still no need for a major concession, especially since the opponent’s offers remain very low for me and have not shown meaningful movement on issueC. Their messages emphasize partnership and urgency, but the actual offers show they are still anchoring hard, so I will keep a strong but not maximal counter.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.725 by conceding issueA and issueB to the opponent’s repeated values while holding issueD at my top value and making only a tiny concession on the most important issueC from valueC to valueD. At t=0.24 there is still time, and the opponent’s offers remain low-utility for me with little real concession beyond occasional movement on issueD, so I should not drop to their latest structure yet. Their messages are cooperative/emotional, but the bidding history shows they are mostly holding firm on issueA/issueB and pushing hard against my preferred issueC.

At t=0.26 there is still substantial time, so I am targeting a high but somewhat more conciliatory utility around 0.70 rather than accepting the opponent’s low-utility offers. The opponent has consistently demanded issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA and recently moved on issueD to valueD, so I concede those lower-weight issues while holding firm near my strongest preference on high-weight issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid while conceding on the opponent’s apparent priorities: they consistently ask for issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA, and recently moved issueD to valueD. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not concede issueC down to valueB/valueA, especially since issueC is my highest-weight issue; their messages are emotional appeals but the offers show the main remaining gap is issueC.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid around 0.725, keeping firmness on the high-weight issueC while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB to the opponent’s repeated pattern. At t=0.30 there is still time, so I should not drop to their low-utility offers; their messages are cooperative appeals, but the actual bids show they mostly hold issueA/issueB at valueA and vary issueC/issueD only modestly.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility while keeping firm on issueC, which is by far my most important issue. The opponent’s actual bids show they strongly prefer issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA and have moved around on issueD, so I can concede the low-weight A/B issues but should not drop issueC to their low values yet at t=0.32. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the numbers show limited concession mainly on issueD, not enough to justify giving up my key issue.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.725 by holding firm on issueC, which is by far my most important issue, while conceding issueA and issueB where my weights are low. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility latest offers; their messages are mostly emotional appeals, while their bids show they remain fixed on issueA/issueB and have only varied issueC/issueD slightly. To avoid simply repeating my last bid, I am offering issueC: valueD with issueD: valueC as a reasonable tradeoff.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 by keeping issueC at my top value, since it is by far my most important issue, while conceding on the low-weight issues A and B. At t=0.36 there is some pressure to move, but not enough to accept their low-value issueD demand; their latest bid finally moved issueC to valueC, and earlier they offered issueD:valueD, so I am combining those signals into a plausible compromise. Their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.725 by holding most value on high-importance issueC/issueD while conceding issueA and issueB to the opponent’s repeated preferred valueA. At t=0.38 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their much lower last offer; their history shows persistence on issueA/issueB and occasional movement on issueD, while their cooperative messages remain cheap talk.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 at this mid-game point, conceding on low-weight issueA and issueB while protecting the most important issueC. The opponent’s offers and messages show repeated pressure and some movement on issueD, including valueD, plus one willingness to place issueC at valueC, so I am combining those signals without trusting the emotional appeals as binding.

I am targeting a strong but slightly concessive bid around the low-0.70 utility range for me, since we are not yet near the deadline but the opponent has stayed firm on issueA and issueB. Their offers suggest they strongly prefer issueA=valueA and issueB=valueA, and they have recently shown willingness on issueD=valueD, so I concede A/B while preserving high value on the most important issueC with a small move from valueC to valueD.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.74 by preserving my most important outcomes on issueC and issueD while conceding the lower-weight issueA and issueB. At t=0.44 there is meaningful time pressure but not a deadline panic; the opponent’s latest valid offer finally moved to my best values on the two highest-weight issues, while their history shows they strongly prefer issueA and issueB at valueA. Their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the actual offers and propose the structure they have just signaled they can accept.
